codeReview.summary: The fix adds more actor mappings and test scaffolding, but it still does not make supervisor-triggered MCP mutations carry trustworthy supervisor provenance. The commit explicitly leaves the MCP provenance gap unresolved, so the original failure is not actually fixed.

issues.description: actorFromCreatedBy now maps CreatedBy="mcp" to ActorMCP, but the feature required supervisor-triggered MCP mutations to preserve supervisor provenance. No caller/session identity is plumbed through the MCP path, and the new TestSupervisorMCPProvenanceGap at internal/service/events_test.go:511-539 explicitly documents that supervisor calls still collapse to the same MCP label instead of ActorSupervisor. The original provenance bug therefore remains.

The new "transport-boundary" tests are still synthetic helper tests: they only call actorFromCreatedBy() and RequiresSupervisorAttention() on hand-built structs. They do not drive the actual CLI/HTTP/MCP handlers, and the idle-backoff test in internal/cli/supervisor_agent_test.go:197-237 only logs the 10-second behavior instead of asserting it, so the requested real regression coverage is still weaker than advertised.

sharedStateObservations.observation: .factory/library/contracts.md still records the MCP provenance problem as a known gap: CreatedBy="mcp" maps to ActorMCP, and only extra session context would let supervisor-triggered MCP writes be identified as ActorSupervisor. That means the mission's authoritative notes still preserve the unresolved behavior instead of the intended fix.

The control-plane-worker procedure requires provenance/wake verification across CLI, HTTP, MCP, and service-generated follow-on paths, but the added tests only exercise pure helpers and log-only state checks. The skill guidance could be clearer that this class of feature needs end-to-end handler coverage, not just mapping assertions.

summary: FAIL: the fix does not close the original MCP provenance gap; it documents the gap and adds helper tests, but does not make supervisor-triggered MCP mutations carry trustworthy supervisor provenance.
